evidence.description: Every CRIT-001..CRIT-014 cites at least one REQ-EDIT or PROP-EDIT that exists in behavioral-spec.md / verification-architecture.md. CRIT-001 → REQ-EDIT-001/010 + PROP-EDIT-021. CRIT-002 → REQ-EDIT-004 + EC-EDIT-001 + PROP-EDIT-023. CRIT-003 → REQ-EDIT-006/007/008 + EC-EDIT-002 + PROP-EDIT-022. CRIT-004 → REQ-EDIT-015..019 + PROP-EDIT-012/013/014/030. CRIT-005 → REQ-EDIT-021/022 + EC-EDIT-006 + PROP-EDIT-016/017. CRIT-006 → REQ-EDIT-023/024 + EC-EDIT-007 + PROP-EDIT-018/019. CRIT-007 → REQ-EDIT-009..013 + PROP-EDIT-024..028. CRIT-008 → REQ-EDIT-014 (AppShell wiring). CRIT-009..012 → adapter and shell signatures from verification-architecture.md §10 / §8. CRIT-013 → NFR-EDIT-005/006/007 + PROP-EDIT-035. CRIT-014 → NFR-EDIT-008 + PROP-EDIT-036/029. The integration-tier PROP coverage matrix in §4 lists supplementary DOM tests for PROP-EDIT-037, 039, 015, 038, 032, 033, 020a, 020b, 034 — collectively covering the integration-tier obligations.

EC-EDIT-001 (rapid burst) is forced into CRIT-002 pass threshold via three named scenarios (at-threshold, one-ms-before, rapid-burst). EC-EDIT-002 (blur-while-saving guard) is forced into CRIT-003 (blur-while-saving spy count === 0). EC-EDIT-006 (whitespace→non-empty reactive transition) is forced into CRIT-005 (reactive transition test passes after two flushSync calls). EC-EDIT-007 (Ctrl+N pane scope) is forced into CRIT-006 (out-of-pane test constructs keydown event targeted at document.body and confirms zero calls). The integration-tier additions cover EC-EDIT-003 (continued typing while save-failed), EC-EDIT-004 (discard mid-flight), EC-EDIT-005 (switching state), EC-EDIT-008 (Ctrl+N while save-failed via PROP-EDIT-020b), EC-EDIT-009 (OS sleep — explicitly accepted as environment-dependent), EC-EDIT-010 (new-note while saving).

Forbidden list explicitly captures Sprint 1 frozen files (types.ts, editorPredicates.ts, editorReducer.ts, debounceSchedule.ts), Sprint 1 test files, Rust handlers (src-tauri/), {@html / innerHTML / outerHTML / insertAdjacentHTML, @testing-library/svelte, writable from svelte/store, and forbids Sprint 2 from importing @tauri-apps/api into pure-core modules. No scope overlap with Sprint 1 detected: Sprint 1 covers types + 3 pure modules; Sprint 2 covers shell adapters + Svelte component + +page.svelte wiring.
